title: Physical and psychosocial benefits of yoga in cancer patients and survivors, a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
headline: Physical and psychosocial benefits of yoga in cancer patients and survivors, a systematic review and meta-anal
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of yoga examined stress & anxiety. Of the outcomes studied in more than three studies among patients with breast cancer, we found large reductions in distress, anxiety, and depression (d = -0.69 to -0.75), moderate reductions in fatigue (d = -0.51), moderate increases in general quality of life, emotional function and social function (d = 0.33 to 0.49), and a small increase in functional well-being (d = 0.31). Yoga appeared to be a feasible intervention and beneficial effects on several physical and psychosocial symptoms were reported.
keyFindings: This study aimed to systematically review the evidence from randomized controlled trials (RCTs) and to conduct a meta-analysis of the effects of yoga on physical and psychosocial outcomes in cancer patients and survivors.

Of the outcomes studied in more than three studies among patients with breast cancer, we found large reductions in distress, anxiety, and depression (d = -0.69 to -0.75), moderate reductions in fatigue (d = -0.51), moderate increases in general quality of life, emotional function and social function (d = 0.33 to 0.49), and a small increase in functional well-being (d = 0.31).
Yoga appeared to be a feasible intervention and beneficial effects on several physical and psychosocial symptoms were reported.
limitations: Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
